refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve or exceed the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a single performance metric: operational availability. It is expressed in a solution-neutral way, specifying what the system must do without implying how. The refinement clarifies the time frame for measurement to align with the constraint and ensures the FR is precise and testable.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve or exceed the customer-defined operational availability target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability
Incorporate real-time system health monitoring and predictive maintenance
Design modular subsystems to isolate faults and enable quick repair
Establish operational procedures and automated controls to minimize downtime
Use robust error detection and recovery mechanisms within system software
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy reduces dependency on single components, supporting independence
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy as a DP supports independence by allowing one component to take over if another fails, minimizing coupling between failure modes.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that redundancy alone may not address all causes of downtime (e.g., operational errors)
Assumes monitoring and operational policies complement redundancy to meet availability target
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and operational controls as secondary DPs to comprehensively address availability beyond hardware redundancy.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety, including failover and redundancy mechanisms

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Used to notify other modules or systems of safety-related events or failover activations

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status data and sends safety status updates

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with redundant components and external safety monitors

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many functions beyond safety, leading to high diffusion of failover logic.
Physical system module; implementing failover here would mix physical and safety logic, causing diffusion.
Focused on remote control; failover is safety-related and would diffuse outside this module.
Most specific and cohesive module for failover implementation; minimal diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Implementing redundant system components for failover within Safety Services provides a focused, cohesive boundary that encapsulates safety-related logic and minimizes coupling with unrelated modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may introduce coupling outside Safety Services.
If failover mechanisms require coordination with Vehicle hardware, some diffusion may occur.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended; current DP aligns well with Safety Services module for containment and minimal diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability' directly addresses the FR of achieving or exceeding the customer-defined operational availability target by providing failover mechanisms to maintain system uptime, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may introduce coupling outside Safety Services.
If failover mechanisms require coordination with Vehicle hardware, some diffusion may occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module has the highest containment score (9) and lowest diffusion score (2), indicating it is the most cohesive and focused boundary for implementing the DP. This minimizes diffusion of failover logic and encapsulates safety-related functions effectively.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible as Safety Services module outputs safety alerts and status updates via defined interfaces, enabling monitoring of failover activations and system availability metrics.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Safety Services IM is clear and well-justified, with minimal overlap or ambiguity in responsibility, supporting traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: No revision recommended; current DP aligns well with Safety Services module for containment and minimal diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong alignment and justification. The DP directly addresses the FR with a clear failover mechanism meeting availability targets. The IM (Safety Services module) is the optimal containment boundary with minimal diffusion risks and clear allocation. Verification feasibility is confirmed, and no revisions are recommended. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further sub-FR derivation.

assumptions: The Safety Services module can fully encapsulate failover logic and related safety functions.
Interfaces with Telemetry, Network Link, and Vehicle hardware are well-defined and manageable to avoid excessive diffusion.
open_risks: Potential coupling with external interfaces (Telemetry, Network Link) may require monitoring during implementation.
Coordination with Vehicle hardware for failover may introduce integration complexity.
evidence_needed: Operational availability metrics post-implementation to confirm availability ≥ 0.97.
Verification reports demonstrating failover activation and safety alert outputs from Safety Services module.
